### What Was Found Across 15+ Tool Calls

| Signal | Strategy | Key Codes Discovered | Count |
|--------|----------|---------------------|-------|
| **Dx** | VSAC ASCO + Optum value sets (43 SNOMED + 12 ICD-10 + ICD-9) | SNOMED `93934004` parent + 28 histology-specific subtypes; ICD-10 `C56.1–C56.9`, `C57.00–C57.02`, `C48.1/2/8`, `Z85.43`; ICD-9 `183.0` | **117** |
| **Chemo** | Server RxNorm sampling → confirmed live codes | `40048` carboplatin, `56946` paclitaxel, `2555` cisplatin, `12574` gemcitabine, `1597582` olaparib | **134** |
| **Procedure** | VSAC bilateral oophorectomy + hysterectomy sets + CPT | SNOMED `83152002`/`86477000`/`116144002` + 17 SNOMED variants; CPT `58150`/`58661`/`58950–58956` + 17 CPT variants | **40** |

### Key Decisions Made

- **FHIR version**: R4 (Azure FHIR Server). No US Core IG loaded — no category filter required on Condition.
- **Code systems live on server**: Conditions → SNOMED + ICD-10-CM + ICD-9-CM simultaneously. MedicationRequests → RxNorm only. Procedures → SNOMED + CPT. Observations → LOINC only. Queries built to match each resource type's actual coding pattern.
- **Ovarian cancer scope**: Includes ovarian primaries (all histologies: serous, mucinous, endometrioid, clear cell, carcinosarcoma, germ cell, sex cord), fallopian tube primaries (C57.x), peritoneal primaries (C48.x) — all are treated and surveilled identically per NCCN guidelines. History code Z85.43 included for patients in remission still under surveillance.
- **Procedures**: Hysterectomy is included (not just oophorectomy) because ovarian cancer surgical staging/debulking almost always includes TAH-BSO — the CPT codes 58950–58956 are the ovarian cancer–specific debulking codes.
- **CA-125 threshold**: `gt35` U/mL (standard clinical threshold); server confirmed 48/224 total CA-125 observations meet this cutoff.
- **Female filter**: `patient.gender=female` applied to all 4 queries.

CA-125 (Cancer Antigen 125) is a tumor marker with a standard upper limit of normal of **35 U/mL**.

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|10334-5&value-quantity=gt35||U/mL&_include=Observation:patient

---

| Parameter | Value | Purpose |
|---|---|---|

- CA-125 is primarily used for **ovarian cancer** monitoring and **endometriosis**.
- You may also encounter CA-125 results under LOINC `83080-0` (body fluid) — add a second query if needed:

Observation?code=http://loinc.org|83080-0&value-quantity=gt35||U/mL&_include=Observation:patient

## FHIR Query: Elevated CA-125 (Cancer Antigen 125) in Serum or Plasma

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|10334-1&value-quantity=gt35||U/mL&status=final,amended,corrected
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|10334-1&value-quantity=gt35||kU/L&status=final,amended,corrected
```

---

### Query Breakdown

| Component | Value | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| **Resource** | `Observation` | Lab result measurements |
| **LOINC Code** | `10334-1` | *Cancer Ag 125 [Units/volume] in Serum or Plasma* — the canonical LOINC for this analyte |
| **Cutoff threshold** | `gt35` | **>35 U/mL** is the universally accepted upper limit of normal (ULN) for CA-125 |
| **Unit — Query 1** | `U/mL` | Most common reporting unit in clinical labs |
| **Unit — Query 2** | `kU/L` | Equivalent unit (1 U/mL = 1 kU/L); same numeric cutoff of 35 applies |
| **Status filter** | `final,amended,corrected` | Excludes preliminary/entered-in-error results to avoid false positives |

---

### Clinical & Specificity Notes

> ⚠️ **CA-125 is sensitive but not cancer-specific.** Elevated results (>35 U/mL) may reflect:
> - **Ovarian cancer** *(primary target — elevated in ~80% of advanced cases)*
> - Endometriosis
> - Uterine fibroids (leiomyomas)
> - Pregnancy (especially first trimester)
> - Pelvic inflammatory disease
> - Other peritoneal or pleural inflammatory conditions

Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C56.9
```

---

### Query Breakdown

#### Query 1 — Oophorectomy (Ovarian Removal)
| SNOMED CT Code | Concept | Clinical Relevance |
|---|---|---|
| `83152002` | Oophorectomy (procedure) | General/parent code — unilateral or bilateral ovary removal |
| `29827000` | Bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy (procedure) | BSO — most common ovarian cancer surgery; removes both ovaries and fallopian tubes |

#### Query 2 — Hysterectomy (Uterine Removal)
| SNOMED CT Code | Concept | Clinical Relevance |
|---|---|---|
| `116140006` | Total hysterectomy (procedure) | Removal of uterus and cervix; general parent code |
| `116144002` | Total abdominal hysterectomy (procedure) | TAH — the open surgical approach; paired with BSO in TAH-BSO for ovarian cancer staging |

---

### Design Notes

- **`status=completed`** — Limits to performed procedures; excludes planned, proposed, or cancelled entries that would inflate the cohort
- **Comma-separated codes** — Applies OR logic within each query, capturing all relevant variants in a single request
- **Two separate queries** — Allows downstream analysis to distinguish patients who had only oophorectomy vs. only hysterectomy vs. both (the TAH-BSO phenotype most specific to ovarian cancer)
- **Known specificity limitation** — Hysterectomy query (~Query 2) will include patients treated for fibroids, endometriosis, or cervical cancer; downstream filtering against a `Condition` resource (e.g., ICD-10 `C56.*` ovarian malignancy) is recommended to refine the ovarian cancer cohort
